analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet argues COVID-19 should not be labeled a 'China virus,' just as Ebola isn't an 'Africa virus.' It stresses that viruses transcend nations and religions, framing the pandemic as humanity vs. nature and warning against discrimination that harms the global fight. Evaluation: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) – Not promoted; no criticism of the West or the US is present. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) – Not promoted; there is no depiction of China's cooperative actions or international leadership, only a general anti-discrimination appeal. CH3 (China has an epic history) – Not relevant. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) – Not promoted; no reference to China’s political system or domestic values. CH5 (The CCP government succeeds) – Not referenced. CH6 (China is interesting culturally/naturally) – Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet is a general anti-stigmatization statement without promoting any listed strategic narrative.
